title: Narrative Reliefs From the SW and NW Corner Pavilions of Angkor Wat
highlights: page of “Narrative Reliefs From the SW and NW Corner Pavilions of Angkor Wat”]![PDF  ...    This study examines the religious and social meaning of the reliefs from the western corner pavilions of the temple of Angkor Wat (12th century AD.). It investigates how visual narrative was articulated by the Khmer, and how the reliefs contributed to the sacredness of the temple and the consolidation Suryavarman II's kingship. In Part I  ...    literature available on the reliefs of the corner pavilion, a detailed examination of the reliefs of the south-western and north-western pavilions is then presented in order to establish the base for their interpretation. title: Notes on Khmer Iconography - Persée
highlights: The Main Images of the Cruciform Pavilion of Angkor Wat: A Reconsideration of Evidence Efforts to discover a certain order or system among the numerous bas-reliefs of Angkor Wat have hitherto scarcely been successful. Neither the eight long panels of the Great Gallery nor the corner pavilions seem to be embellished according to an intelligible concept that could be defined by time, space or content. Przyluski favoured the south door1, Bosch and Maxwell the southeast corner of the Great Gallery as the starting point of a counterclockwise  ...    Coedès, who emphasized that no cogent sequence of panels can be postulated.
